evals.prompt: I'm making a fennel love2d game and I have an enemy spawner module. Every time I ,reload the module my enemy list gets wiped out and all the enemies disappear. The enemies are tables with a metatable that has :draw and :update methods. How do I fix this so the enemy list AND the metatable survive reloads?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend the state module for the enemy list and the hot-table macro for the metatable. Should show the persist macro for the count, hot-table macro for the Enemy metatable, and accessing the enemy list from state.fnl. Should explain WHY metatables break on reload (new table reference).

evals.prompt: I want to start a new love2d game using fennel with interactive development. I've never used fennel before. Walk me through setting up the project from scratch and show me how to get a basic game loop running with hot-reloading.
evals.expected_output: Should recommend the min-love2d-fennel template as the starting point. Should walk through cloning, customizing conf.lua/makefile, the project structure (main.lua, wrap.fnl, mode files), running with love ., using the REPL, and using ,reload to hot-reload modules.

evals.prompt: My fennel love2d game has gameplay working in mode-game.fnl. Now I need to add a pause screen that shows when the player hits escape, and lets them resume or quit. How should I structure this with the mode system?
evals.expected_output: Should create a mode-pause.fnl that returns draw and update callbacks. Should show using set-mode to switch from mode-game to mode-pause on escape, and from mode-pause back to mode-game on resume. Should address state preservation (game state survives in state module while paused).
